messages.cricket.wagon.wheel.desc: Wagon Wheel provides you with a detailed visual presentation of batted shot directions as well as the number of scored runs in a match. Dots on the graph represent the wickets.

messages.profile_badge_top_predictor_info_descrip: To put the Top Predictor badge on your profile, you need to be on the Top predictors leaderboard. You can make it there by making correct predictions on “Who will win?” for matches of any sport. The more you get right, the more points you earn.
messages.editor_badge_text: This badge is awarded to our users who in the role of Editor are in charge of amateur level competitions.
messages.moderator_badge_text: Moderator is responsible for overseeing the chat activities and is the holder of the moderator badge.
messages.contributor_badge_text: As a contributor you improve your Reputation Score by suggesting accurate changes to event details, such as updating the score, scorer, assistant or match start time of a local competition. To claim your badge, you need to either appear on the Top Contributors leaderboard or make at least one accurate contribution and achieve a minimum of 41 Reputation Score.
messages.profile_badges_description: Show some flare and add a badge to your profile!
messages.profile_badge_toast_message_text: This badge is yet to be acquired.
messages.profile_badge_error_message_text: Selected badge was not saved due to a mishap. Try selecting again or switching your selection.
messages.moderators_nickname_edit_restriction: Sorry, chat moderators can’t change their nickname or the badge they’re using. We appreciate your hard work and dedication, but this option is restricted to avoid confusion among other moderators and users.
messages.top_predictors_info_text: The “Who will win” feature challenges your sports knowledge by allowing you to vote on match outcomes. You get points for each correct prediction, which then get tallied up and displayed on the leaderboard next to competitors around the world.
messages.contributors_leaderboard_get_to_top: How to get to the top
messages.contributors_leaderboard_top_text_one: To claim the top spot on the leaderboard, you need to achieve the highest number of accurate and approved suggestions. These suggestions should be made within the last 30 days.
messages.contributors_leaderboard_top_text_two: Suggest accurate scores, the names of scorers and assistants, and the start times of matches for your local club and become a top contributor.
messages.contributors_leaderboard_pts_calculation: The points you see on the leaderboard are the result of multiplying your Reputation Score by the number of your approved suggestions. Striving to achieve a high Reputation Score is key, as it significantly impacts your total points.
messages.contributors_leaderboard_about_rep_score: Your Reputation Score is directly tied to your contribution efforts. Find a local competition of your interest and contribute by suggesting accurate event changes, such as updating the score, scorer, assistant, or match start time. This will improve your score and help you earn a contributor badge for your profile.
messages.contributors_leaderboard_badge_text: It’s up to you to choose the best way. You can earn it by appearing on the Top Contributors leaderboard or making at least one accurate contribution and achieving a minimum of 41 Reputation Score.

messages.monthly_match_views_info_bubble: This graph shows the total number of times your edited matches have been viewed over the past six months, and average match views for all editors. The green line represents your match views, and the yellow line shows average views.
messages.top_editors_leaderboard_info_text: To be the Top Editor, give extra attention to the input time, accuracy, and depth of details you provide for each match. The biggest step you can take towards the top is to make edits as close as possible to the match's live time. All edits made during the live match are worth double points! Also, don't forget the basics. Whether it's a score change or a substitution - the more accurate inputs you make, the more points you earn.

messages.weekly_challenge_waiting: This week's challenge is being prepared and will be ready to view soon. While you wait:
messages.weekly_challenge_sign_in: Sign into your account to join the Weekly Challenge!
messages.weekly_challenge_how_to_body_one: Vote for who you think will win upcoming matches in all your favourite sports - and get points when you guess right.
messages.weekly_challenge_how_to_body_two: Your goal is to collect points, get promoted each week, and make it to #1.
messages.weekly_challenge_what_happens_body_one: A new challenge begins every Monday at 07:00 (UTC) and lasts until the timer runs out.
messages.weekly_challenge_what_happens_body_two: When the timer runs out, those with the most points get promoted to a higher league. Those with the least points get relegated to a lower league, and those in the middle stay put.
messages.weekly_challenge_collecting_points_body_: Collect points by voting on Who will win, Will both teams score and Who will score first for this week’s events.
messages.weekly_challenge_where_to_vote_body: Browse upcoming events in any sport to start voting before the timer for this week runs out!
messages.weekly_challenge_getting_points: You get 1 point every time you vote, and extra points if you get it right.
messages.weekly_challenge_losing_points: You lose 1.5 points every time you get a vote wrong.
messages.weekly_challenge_current_week: Only matches played in the current week count toward your score.
messages.weekly_challenge_later_weeks: Points won or lost for matches beyond the current week get applied during that week’s challenge.
messages.weekly_challenge_name_leaderboard: By tapping your name on the leaderboard, you can view your points and prediction history for this week.
messages.weekly_challenge_info_bottom_sheet_daily: Every day, you can vote on one selected match worth 10x points.
Get 1 point for voting plus 10x points for getting it right
Only lose 1.5 points for getting it wrong
messages.weekly_challenge_active_empty: Events you’ve voted on that are live or upcoming will show up here.
messages.weekly_challenge_finished_empty: Events you've voted on will show up here once they’ve ended.
messages.weekly_challenge_previous_week_empty_des: This is where you can see your results from the previous week.
messages.leaderboard_explainer_bottom_sheet: When the timer runs out, players who finish in the promotion zone advance to a higher league in the next Weekly Challenge, while players in the relegation zone move to a lower league. Those in the middle repeat the same league next week.
messages.timer_explainer_bottom_sheet: The timer shows how much time is left in the current Weekly Challenge. Collect as many points as you can before it runs out!

messages.weekly_streaks_starting_bullet: You start a streak if you get promoted or stay in the same league two weeks in a row
messages.weekly_streaks_staying_bullet: If you're in the lowest league (Novice) and stay there, you don’t start a streak
messages.weekly_streaks_losing_bullet: If you get demoted, your streak resets to 0

messages.event.about1: {homeTeamName} {awayTeamName} live score (and video online live stream*) starts on {startDate} at {startTime} UTC time {hasVenue, select, true {at {stadium} stadium, {city} city, {country}} other {}} in {tournamentName} - {categoryName}.
messages.about_event: Here on Sofascore <link>livescore</link> you can find all {homeTeamName} vs {awayTeamName} previous results sorted by their H2H {sport, select, badminton {matches} snooker {matches} darts {matches} other {matches}}. Links to {homeTeamName} vs. {awayTeamName} video highlights are collected in the Media tab for the most popular {sport, select, badminton {matches} snooker {matches} darts {matches} other {matches}} as soon as video appear on video hosting sites like Youtube or Dailymotion. We're not responsible for any video content, please contact video file owners or hosters for any legal complaints.
messages.event.about3: You can watch {homeTeamName} vs. {awayTeamName} online if you are a registered member of {streamSponsor}, the leading online betting company that covers more than 140,000 live sports events with live betting during the year. If this match is available through {streamSponsor}, you can follow the {sportName} match {homeTeamName} {awayTeamName} on your iPhone, iPad, Android, or Windows phone. It's also easy to find video highlights and news from the most popular sports leagues in the world. Live {streamSponsor} odds are viewable in Sofascore's {sportLink} section. We don't offer a TV schedule here; if you would like to watch this match on TV you'll probably find it on some more popular channels like iTV, BBC, Al Jazeera Sports, Sky Sports, Gol TV, Canal+, SportTV, FOX Soccer, Setanta, ESPN, etc.
messages.event.about4: Sofascore livescore is available as iPhone and iPad app, Android app on Google Play and Windows phone app. You can find us in all stores on different languages as "Sofascore". Install Sofascore app on and follow {homeTeamName} {awayTeamName} live on your mobile!

messages.event.about.new3: Currently, {homeTeamName} rank {homeRank, selectordinal, one {#st} two {#nd} few {#rd} other {#th}}, while {awayTeamName} hold {awayRank, selectordinal, one {#st} two {#nd} few {#rd} other {#th}} position. Looking to compare the best-rated player on both teams? Sofascore's rating system assigns each player a specific rating based on numerous data factors.
messages.event.about.new4: On Sofascore livescore you can find all previous {homeTeamName} vs {awayTeamName} results sorted by their H2H matches. Sofascore also provides the best way to follow the live score of this game with various sports features. Therefore, you can:
messages.event.about.new5: Find out who scored in a live match
messages.event.about.new6: Get real-time information on which team is dominating the match using the Attack Momentum
messages.event.about.new7: Follow detailed statistics such as ball possession, shots, corner kicks, big chances created, cards, key passes, duels and more
messages.event.about.new8: Check all head to head matches – for instance, in the last season {homeTeamName} and {awayTeamName} played {gamesLastSeason} games against each other
messages.event.about.new9: Track all home and away games for each team in the {tournamentName}
messages.event.about.new10: Check out how Sofascore community votes on which team is more likely to win this match.
messages.event.about.new11: All of these features can help you decide on {homeTeamName} vs. {awayTeamName} game prediction. Even though Sofascore doesn't offer direct betting, it provides the best odds and shows you which sites offer live betting. Live {streamSponsor} odds are viewable on Sofascore's <sportlink>{sportName} live score</sportlink> section.

messages.basketball_effective_fg_pct_info: Effective field goal percentage tells you which team is having more success from shooting from the field. In this calculation, a three-pointer is worth one and a half times as much as made two-pointer. Effective field goal percentage is higher for a team that makes a lot of three-pointers compared to a team that mainly relies on two-pointers.
messages.basketball_true_shooting_pct_info: True shooting percentage measures players scoring efficiency. It is calculated by dividing the total points scored by the total shooting attempts, including field goals, three-pointers, and free throws. A higher percentage is desirable as it signifies an effective conversion of scoring opportunities.

messages.tournament.about.tennis.a31: Every team consists of one male and one female player. Each round between two national teams includes 2 singles matches and one mixed doubles match. The teams play against each other in each group using round-robin format. The best teams from each group play against each other in the finals.
messages.tournament.about.tennis.a32: The World Group is the highest-ranked group where 16 best national teams compete for the Davis Cup trophy. Other nations that are not the part of the World Group compete in 3 regional zones. The national teams in each group play against each other and the winners of each group meet in the knockout stage.
messages.tournament.about.tennis.a33: Overall 16 teams qualify for the World Group I and World Group II. There is a promotion and relegation system between the groups that determines the participants for the following year. The best 8 teams compete in a knockout system and each round is consisted of 5 matches. The team that first wins 3 matches qualifies to the next round.

messages.about.ttbl.1: The Tischtennis-Bundesliga (DTTL) is the top German professional table tennis league.
messages.about.ecl.1: <bold>European Champions League (ECL)</bold> is the seasonal table tennis competition for the highest ranked European club teams. The competition starts in September and usually ends in May.
messages.about.ecl.2: The league is completed in two stages. The first stage is the group matches while the second stage is the straight knock-out stage. Here you can follow the results in each group, as well as the knockout stage.
messages.about.wcl.1: Women’s Champions League was first introduced in 2005/2006 season. The competition is part of European Champions League.
messages.about.wcl.2: The league is completed in two stages. The first stage is the group matches while the second stage is the knock-out stage. Here you can check all the previous and upcoming matches and also follow the results in each group, as well as the knockout stage.

messages.about.tt.ettu.men.1: The ETTU Cup, the second most important continental tournament for clubs in European table tennis, right after the <ecllink>European Champions League</ecllink>.
messages.about.tt.ettu.men.2: The competition is organised in three stages. First and second stage are group matches, while third stage is straight knock out system.
messages.about.tt.ettu.men.3: ETTU Cup standings and knockout stage tables are available here, where you can follow them live.
messages.about.tt.ettu.women.1: The ETTU Women Cup is the second most important continental tournament for clubs in European table tennis, right after the <ecllink>European Champions League for women</ecllink>.
messages.about.tt.ettu.women.2: The competition is organised in three stages. First and second stage are group matches, while third stage is a knock out system.
messages.about.tt.ettu.women.3: Here you can check ETTU Women Cup standings, all the previous and upcoming matches and also follow the results in each group, as well as the knockout stage.

messages.atp.about.p1: The ATP also known as The <bold>Association of Tennis Professionals</bold> is the governing body of the men's professional tennis circuits - the ATP Tour, the ATP Challenger Tour and the ATP Champions Tour.
messages.atp.about.p2: The ATP Tour comprises ATP Masters 1000, ATP 500, and ATP 250. The ATP also oversees the <catlink>ATP Challenger Tour</catlink> and the ATP Champions Tour for seniors.
messages.atp.about.p3: The <ranklink>ATP Rankings</ranklink> are used for determining qualification for entry in all tournaments for both singles and doubles. Within the ATP Rankings period consisting of the past year, points are accumulated, with the exception of those for the ATP Finals, whose points are dropped following the last ATP event of the year.
messages.wta.about.p1: The WTA Tour is a worldwide top-tier tennis tour for women organized by the Women's Tennis Association.
messages.wta.about.p2: The WTA Tour comprises Grand Slam tournaments, Year-ending WTA Tour Championships, Premier tournaments and International tournaments.
messages.wta.about.p3: The WTA Rankings are the ratings defined by the Women's Tennis Association. Sofascore keeps the ranking list updated, therefore you can always <ranklink>check current official WTA rankings</ranklink>.
messages.challenger.about.p1: The ATP Challenger Tour is a series of international men's professional tennis tournaments. The Challenger Tour events are the second-highest tier of tennis competition, after the <atplink>ATP Tour</atplink>.
messages.itf-men.about.p1: The <bold>ITF Men's World Tennis Tour</bold> is a series of professional tennis tournaments held around the world that are organized by the International Tennis Federation. The results of ITF tournaments are incorporated into the <ranklink>ATP Ranking</ranklink>, which enables professionals to progress to the <challengerlink>ATP Challenger Tour</challengerlink> and <mainlink>ATP Tour</mainlink>.
messages.itf-men.about.p2: The Tour offers approximately 550 tournaments across 70 countries.
messages.itf-women.about.p1: The <bold>ITF Women's World Tennis Tour</bold> is a series of professional tennis tournaments run by the International Tennis Federation for female professional tennis players. The ITF Women's World Tennis Tour provides entry-level and mid-level professional tournaments. The results of ITF tournaments are incorporated into the <ranklink>WTA Ranking</ranklink>, which enables professionals to progress through to the elite levels of women’s professional tennis.
messages.itf-women.about.p2: The ITF Women's World Tennis Tour offers approximately 500 tournaments across 65 countries.
messages.grand-slam.about.p1: The <bold>Grand Slam</bold> tournaments, also referred to as <bold>majors</bold>, are the world's four most important annual tennis events. The Grand Slam schedule consists of the Australian Open (<aomen>men's</aomen> and <aowomen>women's</aowomen> singles), the French Open (<rgmen>men's</rgmen> and <rgwomen>women's</rgwomen> singles), Wimbledon (<wimwomen>ladies' </wimwomen> and <wimmen>gentlemen's</wimmen> singles) and the US Open (<usmen>men's</usmen> and <uswomen>women's</uswomen> singles).
messages.grand-slam.about.p2: Grand Slam tournaments are not operated by the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) or the Women's Tennis Association (WTA), but by the International Tennis Federation (ITF). However, the <atplink>ATP</atplink> and <wtalink>WTA</wtalink> do award ranking points based on a player's performance.

messages.tv_schedule_about_body_1: The TV Schedule page helps you find out where and when to watch your favourite live sports events. See the full broadcast lineup for today and the upcoming days, all in one place.
messages.tv_schedule_about_body_2: You can check schedules and follow a variety of sports, including <a1>football</a1>, <a2>basketball</a2>, <a3>tennis</a3>, <a4>baseball</a4>, and many others across different competitions and countries.
messages.tv_schedule_about_body_3: Each listed event includes TV channels, streaming options, and event start times, so you can easily plan what to watch. Tapping on any match will open its live score page on Sofascore, where you’ll find real-time results, detailed statistics, player ratings, and key match moments.

messages.striking_per_round_info_text: Striking per Round illustrates how each fighter strikes on a round-by-round basis. Significant Strikes Average shows landed to all strikes success ratio. Sig. Strikes Landed by Zones (Avg.) displays body parts struck by various strike actions.
messages.significant_strikes_info_text: Significant Strikes breaks down all significant strikes: total, by position, or by the target. Total Strikes explains how many strikes were successful. Strikes by Position refers to all strikes that fighters struck from different positions during a fight. The following section, Strikes by Zones, displays the number of strikes that struck various body parts, as shown on the map. Knockdowns indicate how many times a fighter knocked down an opponent on the ground.

messages.mma_grappling_per_min_description: Grappling per 15 Min refers to techniques used to gain an advantage over an opponent without striking them: takedowns, submissions, and transitions. Takedowns Average shows a fighter's efficiency in forcing his opponent to the ground from standing. Submissions Average shows a fighter's efficiency in yielding the opponent and winning the match. Below is Transitions Average, which displays a fighter's ability to move from one grappling hold or position to another.
messages.mma_position_stats_description: Position Statistics give insight into the fighter's offensive approach. Each fighter's time at a distance or ground on a round-by-round basis is calculated within Avg. Time in Position per Round. The amount of time spent on maintaining advantageous positioning when on the ground is visible in Control in Ground Position.
messages.mma_grappling_description: Grappling refers to techniques used to gain an advantage over an opponent without striking them: takedowns, transitions, and submissions. Takedowns show a fighter's efficiency in forcing his opponent to the ground from standing. The following section, Transitions, displays a fighter's ability to move from one grappling hold or position to another. Finally, the Submissions section gives information on the fighter's number of attempts to yield the opponent and win the match.
messages.mma_position_stats_description_live: Position Statistics give insight into the fighter's offensive approach. Time in Position measures the fighter's time at a distance, clinch, or ground during a fight. The fighter's ability to maintain advantageous positioning on the ground is measured by Control in Ground Position.

messages.widgets_players_body: Track individual performances using detailed metrics that show exactly how each player contributed. From basic info to in-depth match stats, it’s easy to spot standout moments or quiet games.

messages.seo_about_football_player_of_the_season:
Sofascore Player of the Season: Recognising statistical excellence in sport

The Sofascore Player of the Season award celebrates the most consistently—and objectively—excellent players in world football. Unlike subjective accolades, this award is purely data-driven, honouring players who achieve their league or tournament’s highest average Sofascore Rating at the end of the season. Featuring a unique design inspired by the legendary Zico, Sofascore’s Player of the Season is the most transparent award in football.

How winners are determined

Sofascore crowns Player of the Season winners in over 150 global leagues and tournaments annually. Winners are determined by a sophisticated algorithm that processes thousands of data points from every match. Every action on the pitch—goals, assists, passes, interceptions, successful dribbles, to name just a few—contributes to a player’s average season rating. This objective approach ensures that the Player of the Season is a true reflection of sustained excellence, rewarding those who perform at the highest level week in, week out.

Global recognition across major leagues

Sofascore’s Player of the Season award is presented in over 150 professional football leagues worldwide. Physical trophies are sent to winners in the world's most elite major-league competitions, while in-app badges recognise winners across all participating leagues and tournaments.

Key leagues and tournaments include:

  • Premier League (England)
  • La Liga (Spain)
  • Bundesliga (Germany)
  • UEFA Champions League
  • Serie A (Italy)
  • Ligue 1 (France)
  • Saudi Pro League (Saudi Arabia)
  • Brasileirão Betano (Brazil)
  • MLS (USA)
  • FIFA World Cup

Notable past Player of the Season winners:

  • Lionel Messi
  • Cristiano Ronaldo
  • Kylian Mbappé
  • Joshua Kimmich
  • Antoine Griezmann
  • Raphinha

messages.squad_size_fantasy_rule:
To join a competition, create a squad of 15 players consisting of:{br}{br}
  • 2 Goalkeepers
  • 5 Defenders
  • 5 Midfielders
  • 3 Forwards
messages.player_positions_fantasy_rule: As we all know, some players play in multiple positions, and their roles can change throughout the season. Due to our Fantasy format and scoring system, we have to restrict each player to a single-player position at the start of the Fantasy season based on his previous seasons.{br}{br} That means that each player will score points for the position he is given at the start of the season, not by the position he has in each starting lineup throughout the season.
messages.players_per_team_fantasy_rule: You can select up to 3 players from a single team.
messages.budget_fantasy_rule: You can select your initial squad with a budget of €100 million. The unused budget will be put into your bank, and available to you during transfers.
messages.starting_11_fantasy_rule:
Your squad will always consist of 15 players. Out of those 15 players, you need to select your starting 11 for each round. Those are the only players for which you will earn Fantasy points.{br}{br} Your starting 11 has to have:{br}{br}
  • exactly 1 goalkeeper
  • at least 3 defenders
  • at least 3 midfielders
  • at least 1 forward
messages.selecting_a_captain_fantasy_rule: Just like every real team needs a captain, so does your Fantasy team. The Fantasy points of your captain will be doubled in every round.
messages.transfers_fantasy_rule: After selecting your squad, you can make transfers by dropping players and selecting replacements while staying within your team's budget. Transfers can be made within the designated transfer period for each round.{br}{br}The transfer period opens at the start of a round and ends when the transfer deadline is reached. You can make unlimited transfers at no cost until the transfer deadline of the round in which you join a Fantasy competition.{br}{br}You will receive 1 free transfer for each round. If you don't use your free transfer, it will be carried over to the next round. You can have a maximum of 2 free transfers per round.{br}The only exception is the MLS competition. You get 5 free transfers each round, but they don't carry over.{br}{br}If you wish to make additional transfers, you may do so by accepting a deduction of 5 points per additional transfer from your total score for the round.{br}{br} You are limited to 15 transfers in any single round. However, this limit doesn't apply when playing a Quick Fix or a Rebuild Squad token.
messages.live_squad_changes_fantasy_rule:
While the transfer deadline prevents you from making additional transfers in the current round, this restriction doesn't apply to your starting lineup.{br}{br}Players become locked once their first match within a round starts. As long as you have at least one unlocked player in your squad, you may make the following changes:{br}{br}
  • Change your captain: You can select any unlocked player as your new captain.
  • Make substitutions: You can swap an unlocked player from your bench into your starting lineup, as long as you follow the position rules.
messages.fantasy_rescheduled_interrupted_matches_: These rules explain how squad locking and points work when matches are rescheduled or interrupted.
messages.fantasy_rescheduled_matches_rule:
A postponed match is moved to a future round.{br}{br}Players will score points in the round when the match is played, not the original one. Because of this, some teams may play two matches in the same round (a double round). In that case, a player earns points from both matches.{br}{br}When a match starts:{br}{br}
  • If a player is on the bench, you can no longer move them into your starting lineup.
  • If you move the captaincy off a player who has already played, they lose their double points bonus. Their score reverts to a standard single score, and you can't reassign the captaincy to them for that round.
messages.fantasy_interrupted_matches_rule: If a match is interrupted or abandoned, it remains tied to the original round, even if it's completed at a later date.{br}{br}If a player was in your starting lineup at the time of the match, you will receive their points. Even if you later remove the player from your squad, those points still count.
messages.tokens_fantasy_rule: You can use tokens to improve your team's performance during the season. Only one token can be used during a single round.
messages.fantasy_triple_captain_token_rule: Instead of receiving double points for your captain, you'll receive triple points when using the Triple Captain token. This token must be activated before the transfer deadline of the round. Once active, it can be moved to any unlocked player in your squad during the round, just like a regular captain.{br}{br}You receive one Triple Captain token per Fantasy season.
messages.fantasy_quick_fix_token_rule: The Quick Fix token allows you to make unlimited transfers to improve your squad for one round only. This is particularly useful when multiple players are unavailable due to cup matches or international tournaments.{br}{br}After the round is over, all changes made using the Quick Fix token will be reset, and your previous squad will be reinstated.{br}{br}You receive one Quick Fix token per Fantasy season.
messages.fantasy_rebuild_squad_token_rule: Use the Rebuild Squad token to make unlimited transfers during a single round. Unlike the Quick Fix, these changes are permanent, giving you a completely new squad for the remainder of the season.{br}{br}Two Rebuild Squad tokens are available per season: one for the first half of the season and a second for the second half.
messages.fantasy_scoring_rule: Points are based on real-life performance across 30 statistical categories, depending on position. Stats come from our data providers and are verified after every match. It can take up to 24 hours after a match for points to be finalised.
messages.sofascore_rating_fantasy_rule: Players also earn points based on their Sofascore Rating.
messages.goals_fantasy_rule: Points for goals are awarded based on player position.
messages.assists_fantasy_rule: Points for assists are awarded based on player position.
messages.assists_fantasy_rule_2: To earn points in this category, a player has to be credited with the assist in the official match report by our data providers.
messages.own_goals_fantasy_rule: Players receive -2 points per own goal.
messages.second_yellow_card_fantasy_rule: Given that a second yellow card in the early stages of a match leaves your team shorthanded for a longer period of time, we give more negative Fantasy points the earlier a player gets it:
messages.red_card_fantasy_rule: An early dismissal leaves a team shorthanded for longer. The earlier the red card, the bigger the penalty.
messages.appearance_fantasy_rule_description: Players earn 1 point for appearing in a match. If they play 60 minutes or more, that goes up to 2 points.
messages.clean_sheets_fantasy_rule: Goalkeepers and defenders earn 4 points for not conceding a goal, provided they play at least 60 minutes.
messages.goals_conceded_fantasy_rule: Goalkeepers and defenders lose 1 point for every 2 goals conceded.
messages.punches_plus_high_claims_fantasy_rule: A punch is when a goalkeeper clears a high ball with a clenched fist. A high claim is when they successfully catch a ball out of the air. Points are awarded for the combined total of both, at a rate of 1 point per 2 actions (rounded down).
messages.long_balls_fantasy_rule: Players receive 1 point for completing at least 3 accurate long balls with a success rate of 60% or higher.
messages.clearances_fantasy_rule: A clearance occurs when a player kicks the ball away from their own goal area to eliminate danger. Players receive 1 point per 5 clearances in a single match.
messages.shots_blocked_fantasy_rule: When outfield players block an attempt on goal, they receive 1 point per 2 shots blocked. Attempts that go wide do not count.
messages.interceptions_fantasy_rule: An interception occurs when a player cuts off an opponent’s pass. Players will receive 1 point for every 3 interceptions.
messages.tackles_won_fantasy_rule: A tackle is won when a player connects with the ball and takes control away from the opposition player. If the ball goes to a teammate or safely out of play, that also counts as a successful tackle.{br}{br}Players will receive 1 point for every 3 tackles won.
messages.duels_won_fantasy_rule_description: A duel is a 50/50 contest between two opposing players. Players receive 1 point for winning at least 3 duels with a success rate of 50% or higher.
messages.passing_fantasy_rule: Players will receive 1 point for completing at least 40 passes with a success rate of 90% or higher.
messages.key_passes_fantasy_rule: A key pass is one that leads directly to a shot attempt, but doesn't result in a goal. Players will receive 1 point for every 2 key passes.
messages.successful_dribbles_fantasy_rule_descrip: A successful dribble occurs when a player beats an opponent while retaining possession of the ball. Players will receive 1 point for completing at least 3 successful dribbles with a success rate of 60% or higher.

messages.sofascore_rating_basketball_box_score: Ratings reflect a player’s overall game performance, and are calculated after a minimum of 6 minutes played.
messages.rating_general_text: A statistical indicator that provides an insight into player’s performance.<break></break> Hundreds of data points are calculated and presented as a single number. Ratings are updated more than 60 times combined during and after the match.
messages.average_player_rating_text: Average Sofascore Rating represents the player’s statistical rating over a period of time across multiple matches. It’s calculated by summing up the player’s individual Sofascore Rating from each match and then dividing the total by the number of matches.
messages.average_team_rating_text: Average Sofascore Rating represents the team’s statistical rating over a period of time across multiple matches. It’s calculated by summing up the team’s individual Sofascore Rating from each match and then dividing the total by the number of matches.
messages.average_team_rating_match: Average Sofascore Rating represents the team's statistical rating for this match. It's calculated by summing up the players' individual Sofascore Ratings and then dividing the total by the number of rated players within the team.
messages.rating_team_of_the_week: Team of the week features the highest-rated players in certain positions for a specific round. It’s based on single-match player ratings for the same round within a particular competition.
messages.rating_team_of_the_season: Team of the season features the highest-rated players in certain positions for the specific season. It’s based on the seasonal average player ratings within a particular competition.

messages.fantasy_cwc_player_position_rule: As we all know, some players play in multiple positions, and their roles can change throughout the tournament. Due to our Fantasy format and scoring system, we assign each player a single position at the start of the tournament based on their typical role in recent competitions.{br}{br}That means that each player will score points for the position he is given at the start of the tournament, not by the position he has in each starting lineup throughout the tournament.
messages.fantasy_cwc_schedule_rule: Each round of the tournament has a defined start date and time. These timeframes determine when your team must be set and when transfers are no longer available.
messages.fantasy_cwc_transfers_rule: The number of transfers allowed and the limit on players from the same team vary by tournament stage. These limits apply throughout each respective round.
messages.fantasy_transfers_before_final_and_3rd_p: Before the final and 3rd place match
messages.tournament_position_fantasy_rule: Players are assigned a single position based on their most common role in recent competitions. They will score points for that position throughout the tournament, even if their role changes in real life.
messages.tournament_budget_fantasy_rule: You can select your initial squad within a budget of €100 million. Any unused money is automatically placed in your bank, and will be available to you for future transfers.{br}{br}Once the group phase ends, your budget increases by €5 million. This happens automatically when knockout stage transfers open.
messages.starting_eleven_fantasy_rule:
Your squad always consists of 15 players. Each round, you select 11 to go out onto the pitch, while the remaining 4 players stay on the bench. Only the points won by your 11 fielded players at the end of the round will count towards your score.{br}{br}Your starting 11 has to have:{br}{br}
  • exactly 1 goalkeeper
  • at least 3 defenders
  • at least 3 midfielders
  • at least 1 forward.
messages.choosing_a_captain_fantasy_rule: Pick one player from your starting 11 to be the captain. His points will be doubled for that round.{br}{br}Not happy with your pick? You can switch captaincy to any player whose match hasn't kicked off yet. It's one of the easiest ways to gain an edge on your rivals.{br}{br}Keep in mind: once you change captaincy, you can't switch back.
messages.subs_during_matches_fantasy_rule:
If you’re not happy with how a starting player is performing, you can take him out of the team and sub in someone from the bench. The conditions for this are:{br}{br}
  • The bench player’s match hasn’t started yet
  • The substitution follows the formation restrictions above
    • {br}{br}Points won by players who have been subbed off do not count towards your score.{br}{br}Keep in mind: once you sub out an active player, you can't put him back in.
messages.tournament_tokens_fantasy_rule: Tokens are special one-time boosts you can use to get more points from your squad. For this tournament you can use only one, so choose your moment carefully.
messages.triple_captain_token_fantasy_rule: Your captain normally scores double points. With the Triple Captain active, they score triple instead.{br}{br}Activate this token before the round's transfer period ends. Once active, you can reassign it to any unlocked player in your squad—the same way you'd pick a regular captain.
messages.tournament_deadlines_fantasy_rule: Each round has a deadline 30 minutes before the first match. After that, no more transfers can be made. Turn on notifications to get reminders for every round.{br}{br}There are 8 rounds in total, with the final and the 3rd-place match making up the last round.

messages.tournament_transfers_fantasy_rule: The number of transfers you can make—and how many players you can pick from the same team—depends on the stage of the tournament.
messages.player_prices_fantasy_rule: Player prices change based on form, performance, and demand. When a player is performing well, more managers add them to their squad, driving the price up. The more managers sell a player, the more the price drops. Prices update after each transfer deadline and at the end of every round.

messages.schema_description_rankings_fifa: FIFA rankings are a system for scoring the performance of national football teams, based on points awarded for wins and losses in matches. The rankings are determined using an Elo rating system, which considers a team's relative strength, the importance of the match, and the result against the expected result. This system is used for seeding teams in tournaments, like the World Cup, to ensure fairer group draws.
messages.schema_description_rankings_uefa: The UEFA club coefficient rankings are based on the results of all European clubs in UEFA club competition. The association club coefficient or country rankings take into account the results of all clubs from each association and are used to determine the number of entries an association is granted for forthcoming seasons. The club coefficient rankings take into account the results of each club in UEFA club competition and are used to determine a club's seeding in club competition draws. The coefficients – from which the rankings are formed and which are based on performances over the last five seasons – are be updated directly after each completed round of UEFA club competition matches.
messages.schema_description_rankings_utr-men: The UTR Rating, also known as the Universal Tennis Rating, was created by UTR Sports (formerly known as Universal Tennis) as a global rating system that promotes fair and competitive play across the tennis world. One match result is all it takes to receive a projected UTR Rating. After approximately five matches, the rating becomes fully reliable. As matches are played and entered into the system, a player's UTR Rating will increase or decrease over time. For each match, the algorithm calculates a match rating and a match weight for each player.
messages.schema_description_rankings_utr-women: The UTR Rating, also known as the Universal Tennis Rating, was created by UTR Sports (formerly known as Universal Tennis) as a global rating system that promotes fair and competitive play across the tennis world. One match result is all it takes to receive a projected UTR Rating. After approximately five matches, the rating becomes fully reliable. As matches are played and entered into the system, a player's UTR Rating will increase or decrease over time. For each match, the algorithm calculates a match rating and a match weight for each player.
messages.schema_description_rankings_rugby-union: The World Rugby Rankings is a ranking system for national teams in rugby union, managed by World Rugby, the sport's governing body. The teams of World Rugby's member nations are ranked based on their game results, with the most successful teams being ranked highest. A point system is used, with points being awarded on the basis of the results of World Rugby-recognized international matches. Rankings are based on the team's performance, with more recent results and more significant matches being more heavily weighted to help reflect the current competitive state of a team.
messages.schema_description_rankings_atp: ATP rankings are the merit-based method used by Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP) for determining the qualification for entry as well as the seeding of players in all singles and doubles tournaments. Ranking points are awarded according to the stage of tournament reached, and the prestige of the tournament, with the four Grand Slam tournaments awarding the most points. The rankings are updated every Monday, and points are dropped 52 weeks after being awarded (with the exception of the ATP Finals, from which points are dropped on the Monday following the last ATP Tour event of the following year).
messages.schema_description_rankings_wta: The WTA rankings are based on a rolling 52-week, cumulative system. A player's ranking is determined by her results at a maximum of 18 tournaments (or 19 if she competed in the WTA Finals) for singles and 12 for doubles. Points are awarded based on how far a player advances in a tournament. The basis for calculating a player's ranking are those tournaments that yield the highest ranking points during the rolling 52-week period. In order to appear on the WTA rankings, players must earn ranking points in at least three tournaments, or a minimum of 10 singles ranking points or 10 doubles ranking points in one or more tournaments.

messages.schema_description_rankings_fip-ranking-: The FIP Ranking is the official global ranking system for men's professional padel, maintained by the International Padel Federation. Points are awarded based on results in FIP-sanctioned tournaments, weighted by event level, with higher-tier events such as Premier Padel Majors awarding the most points. The ranking uses a rolling 52-week system, meaning points expire one year after they are earned. It is used to determine seedings and entry into tournaments, as well as eligibility for international competition.

The FIP Ranking is the official global ranking system for women's professional padel, maintained by the International Padel Federation. Points are awarded based on results in FIP-sanctioned tournaments, weighted by event level, with higher-tier events such as Premier Padel Majors awarding the most points. The ranking uses a rolling 52-week system, meaning points expire one year after they are earned. It is used to determine seedings and entry into tournaments, as well as eligibility for international competition.
